reasoning: Sheikh Hasina fled to India in August 2024 after being ousted, and faces severe legal charges and an extradition request (filed December 2024) from the interim Yunus government. Extraditing a former head of state is a lengthy and politically fraught process, and India has shown no willingness to send her back to face trial; they are much more likely to continue harboring her or facilitate her move to a third country. While Hasina issued rhetorical statements in April 2025 vowing to return, there is no practical or political mechanism for her to do so safely without a massive, sudden counter-coup, which currently shows no signs of materializing. Given that her return would almost certainly result in immediate arrest, and considering the very narrow 50-day window (May 12 to July 1, 2025), the probability of her entering Bangladeshi territory during this specific period is exceptionally low. I am forecasting the minimum allowed probability of 5%.
